title: Specialized Information and Referral * LGBTQ2+ Issues - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Specialized Information and Referral * LGBTQ2+ Issues 6 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - Assistive Technology Issues Blindness Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions Deafness Disabilities Issues Down Syndrome Mental Health Issues Older Adult/Aging Issues Immigration Issues Abused Lesbians/Gay Individuals Abused Transgender Individuals LGBTQ2+ Issues Transgender Individuals Offender/Ex-Offender Issues Native American Community AIDS/HIV Issues Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF BASIC RIGHTS OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Builds and supports political policies that promote equality for lesbian, gay, bisexual, and transgender Oregonians. Provides various services including information and referral to legal services, youth mentorship programs, education and outreach to the business community, political advocacy, and offers volunteer opportunities. Eligibility: Individuals with interest in LGBTQ2+ issues Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 222-6151 Visit Website More Details MID-WILLAMETTE TRANSGENDER SUPPORT NETWORK CASE MANAGEMENT AND RESOURCE DISPATCHING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides advocacy and resource navigation including finding gender-affirming healthcare providers, food, housing, medical supplies and obtaining identification documents. 260 SW Madison Avenue Suite 110 Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Individuals who are queer, transgender, non-binary, intersex, gender non-conforming, or intersex and their parents/guardians or close relatives or kinship Hours: Sunday-Friday noon-5pm or by appointment Email (541) 264-1973 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PFLAG LGBTQ2+ SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information about support groups and specialized information and referral through local chapters of the organization. 1625 K Street NW Suite 700 Washington, DC 200065112 Eligibility: Individuals who identify as LGBTQ2+ and their friends and families Hours: Not applicable Email (202) 467-8180 Get Directions Visit Website More Details Q CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a community event space and offers groups oriented around topics such as sexual orientation, gender identity, addiction and mental health supports, and other topics. Groups are offered both in-person or online. Also provides specialized information and referral. 4115 N Mississippi Avenue Portland, OR 97217 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: By appointment only Email (503) 234-7837 Get Directions Visit Website More Details THE HARBOR Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a day center, domestic and sexual violence advocacy, culturally specific LGBTQ2+ and Latinx services, relocation assistance, transportation to a safe location, accompaniment to medical or forensic exams, assistance applying for Crime Victims Compensation, and information and referral to community agencies. Provides educational programs. These programs focus on raising awareness about the dynamics of domestic and sexual violence, understanding trauma, and promoting prevention strategies within the community. Currently accepting volunteers. 801 Commercial Street Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Age 14 and older Hours: Advocacy Office: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 9am-4pm, Wednesday 9am-noon, closed 1st Wednesday of every month. Online Chat and Text: Monday-Friday 10am-5pm Email (503) 325-5735 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRANSITION HEALTH COMPREHENSIVE WELLNESS AND QUEER MEDICINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides gender-affirming healthcare and health education to queer, transgender, and intersex communities. Services include hormone therapy, electrolysis, and body image resurces, as well as assistance with name and indentification changes. 1590 Willamette Street Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: No standard hours Email (541) 357-7594 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MID-WILLAMETTE TRANSGENDER SUPPORT NETWORK CASE MANAGEMENT AND RESOURCE DISPATCHING Q CENTER Q CENTER THE HARBOR THE HARBOR TRANSITION HEALTH COMPREHENSIVE WELLNESS AND QUEER MEDICINE
